text: Unit tests check individual pieces of code, like a single function or method, to ensure they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of a system work together as expected. A popular approach is test-driven development (TDD), where developers write tests before writing the actual code. This might seem backwards at first, but it helps clarify requirements and prevent bugs early on. Ultimately, all types of testing give the team confidence. When a codebase has thorough tests, developers can make changes or add new features without worrying about breaking existing functionality.
